title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
excerpt: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.
content_html:
For months, AI giants have devised special vetted programs and strict guardrails to limit the use of their models by malicious hackers. But these limits are now hindering the work of legitimate network defenders, as well as that of offensive cybersecurity researchers. 

In June, the U.S. government slapped export control restrictions on Anthropic’s much-hyped AI models Mythos and Fable. The move was prompted at least in part by a report that claimed it was possible to bypass the models’ guardrails designed to prevent users from using them to build and execute malicious cyberattacks.

Regardless of whether the incident was really motivated by fears of a jailbreak, the fact is that Anthropic has repeatedly marketed Mythos as some kind of doomsday cybermachine that can only be given to carefully vetted users, and even then with strict guardrails in place. (The export controls on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 have since been lifted. Fable 5 returned to general access on July 1; Mythos 5 has been reintroduced only to vetted U.S. organizations as part of the government’s review process.)

That kind of gatekeeping isn’t unique to Mythos. Both Anthropic, with its other models, and OpenAI offer cybersecurity researchers programs they can apply to get vetted and — if approved — access models with fewer cybersecurity restrictions: OpenAI’s Trusted Access for Cyber program and Anthropic’s Cyber Verification Program. 

These guardrails have been widely criticized, particularly by researchers whose job is to find unknown vulnerabilities in systems and devise ways to exploit them before criminals do.

During a recent appearance on a cybersecurity podcast, Mark Dowd, a well-known security researcher, said that, “it’s not really comfortable to me that these random large companies are making arbitrary decisions about what is safe in security and what’s not.”

Dowd has spent decades finding and selling “zero-days” — previously unknown software flaws and the exploits that take advantage of them — to Western governments, rather than reporting them to the software makers so they get patched. Governments pay a premium for vulnerabilities precisely because they stay open, which is useful for intelligence operations.

Dowd admitted his work may make him biased, but he isn’t alone. Several people who work in offensive cybersecurity — they proactively probe systems for weaknesses — described to TechCrunch how they use AI tools and deal with their guardrails. 

Chris Anley, the chief scientist at security consulting giant NCC Group, said that asking an AI model to try to exploit a bug is a key step in confirming it’s a real vulnerability worth fixing. But if a guardrail prompts the model to refuse to answer the question outright, the guardrail hurts defenders, he said.

“This is where the whole offensive versus defensive and guardrails part comes in, because ‘fix this code’ as a prompt is both an essential mechanism for defense but also a roadmap for finding critical vulnerabilities in the code base,” said Anley. “So at the same time, the same tool is both an offensive tool and a defensive tool, and the two can’t really be unpicked.”

It’s “like a hammer,” he continued. “You can’t build a house without a hammer. It’s definitely a tool but it’s also irreducibly a weapon as well.”

When he and his colleagues run into such a roadblock, they sometimes fall back on open source AI models that come with no guardrails at all.

Paolo Stagno, the chief technology officer at Crowdfense, a well-known company that develops, acquires, and sells unknown vulnerabilities to government agencies, agreed with Dowd, saying AI companies “essentially treat customers like children who need babysitting” with their vetted programs and guardrails. 

Stagno said he and his colleagues do use frontier models — but only for reverse engineering. They avoid using AI to help find vulnerabilities or build exploits, he said, because feeding that work into a cloud-based model risks leaking sensitive vulnerability data or having it absorbed into future training runs. For that step, he said, they use open source models run locally, as they do not rely on sharing data outside of the model. 

Giuseppe Cali, a security researcher who finds zero-days and develops exploits, said guardrails are not impeding his work. That’s because he doesn’t use AI for offensive work; instead, he uses it for initial reverse engineering, to understand the code he’s analyzing, and to build supporting tools. For that, he said, AI tools can speed up the process and allow him to focus on discovering vulnerabilities. 

“I still want to own the actual bug discovery and weaponization myself and that wouldn’t change if all guardrails were lifted tomorrow,” said Cali. “I am jealous of my bugs, and I like this game too much to let models play it for me.”

One researcher at a smartphone-component manufacturer, who spoke on condition of anonymity because he isn’t authorized to talk to the press, said his employer isn’t part of Anthropic’s CVP program and as a result, its tools are barely useful for finding vulnerabilities because the guardrails are too strict.

“If it catches wind we’re doing anything security related, it just stops and isn’t usable,” the person said. 

Chris Thompson — chief executive of cybersecurity firm RemoteThreat and founder of Offensive AI Con, an offensive security and AI-focused event — said that in his experience using the frontier AI models, the guardrails can be inconsistent and work differently every day. That’s true even inside the looser boundaries of Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s vetted programs. 

“I think the practical impact is you spend a lot of time negotiating with the model instead of working on the core security program,” said Thompson. “Instead of analyzing a vulnerability and reasoning through the exploitability, you’re trying to find why you’re getting inconsistent results or why are models over-sanitizing the output.” 

Consequently, researchers rely on or get pushed toward Chinese open source models like GLM — freely downloadable models that can be run locally with no vetting or usage restrictions — said Thompson.

“You have these responsible researchers that are being pushed away from U.S.-governed systems to foreign-owned systems,” he said. “I think it’s more harmful than good to have these guardrails in place.”

Rather than tightening restrictions further, Thompson called for the AI frontier labs to open up their programs, provide responsible access, and hold those who abuse their tools accountable. Otherwise, he argued, defenders will lose the AI race.

“There’s this big storm coming. There’s this big wave of attacks that are going to happen at speed and scale like never before,” said Thompson. “But the same security consulting firms and legit researchers that are trying to make a difference are being stifled right now.”

(The export controls on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 have since been lifted. blocks.html_en: For months, AI giants have devised special vetted programs and strict guardrails to limit the use of their models by malicious hackers. But these limits are now hindering the work of legitimate network defenders, as well as that of offensive cybersecurity researchers.

sentences.en: For months, AI giants have devised special vetted programs and strict guardrails to limit the use of their models by malicious hackers.
sentences.zh: 几个月来，人工智能巨头制定了经过特殊审查的程序和严格的防护措施，以限制恶意黑客对其模型的使用。

sentences.en: But these limits are now hindering the work of legitimate network defenders, as well as that of offensive cybersecurity researchers.
sentences.zh: 但这些限制现在阻碍了合法网络防御者以及进攻性网络安全研究人员的工作。

blocks.html_en: In June, the U.S. government slapped export control restrictions on Anthropic’s much-hyped AI models Mythos and Fable. The move was prompted at least in part by a report that claimed it was possible to bypass the models’ guardrails designed to prevent users from using them to build and execute malicious cyberattacks.

sentences.en: In June, the U.S. government slapped export control restrictions on Anthropic’s much-hyped AI models Mythos and Fable.
sentences.zh: 6 月，美国政府对 Anthropic 大肆宣传的人工智能模型 Mythos 和 Fable 实施了出口管制限制。

sentences.en: The move was prompted at least in part by a report that claimed it was possible to bypass the models’ guardrails designed to prevent users from using them to build and execute malicious cyberattacks.
sentences.zh: 这一举措至少部分是由一份报告推动的，该报告声称可以绕过这些模型的护栏，该护栏旨在防止用户使用它们来构建和执行恶意网络攻击。

blocks.html_en: Regardless of whether the incident was really motivated by fears of a jailbreak, the fact is that Anthropic has repeatedly marketed Mythos as some kind of doomsday cybermachine that can only be given to carefully vetted users, and even then with strict guardrails in place. (The export controls on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 have since been lifted. Fable 5 returned to general access on July 1; Mythos 5 has been reintroduced only to vetted U.S. organizations as part of the government’s review process.)

sentences.en: Regardless of whether the incident was really motivated by fears of a jailbreak, the fact is that Anthropic has repeatedly marketed Mythos as some kind of doomsday cybermachine that can only be given to carefully vetted users, and even then with strict guardrails in place.
sentences.zh: 不管这起事件是否真的是出于对越狱的恐惧，事实是 Anthropic 一再将 Mythos 推销为某种末日网络机器，只能提供给经过仔细审查的用户，即使如此，也有严格的防护措施。

sentences.en: (The export controls on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 have since been lifted.
sentences.zh: （《神鬼寓言 5》和《神话 5》的出口管制现已取消。

sentences.en: Fable 5 returned to general access on July 1; Mythos 5 has been reintroduced only to vetted U.S. organizations as part of the government’s review process.)
sentences.zh: 《神鬼寓言 5》于 7 月 1 日重新开放；作为政府审查流程的一部分，神话 5 仅重新引入经过审查的美国组织。）

blocks.html_en: That kind of gatekeeping isn’t unique to Mythos. Both Anthropic, with its other models, and OpenAI offer cybersecurity researchers programs they can apply to get vetted and — if approved — access models with fewer cybersecurity restrictions: OpenAI’s Trusted Access for Cyber program and Anthropic’s Cyber Verification Program.

sentences.en: That kind of gatekeeping isn’t unique to Mythos.
sentences.zh: 这种把关并不是神话独有的。

sentences.en: Both Anthropic, with its other models, and OpenAI offer cybersecurity researchers programs they can apply to get vetted and — if approved — access models with fewer cybersecurity restrictions: OpenAI’s Trusted Access for Cyber program and Anthropic’s Cyber Verification Program.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 及其其他模型和 OpenAI 都提供网络安全研究人员计划，他们可以申请进行审查，如果获得批准，则可以访问网络安全限制较少的模型：OpenAI 的可信网络访问计划和 Anthropic 的网络验证计划。

blocks.html_en: These guardrails have been widely criticized, particularly by researchers whose job is to find unknown vulnerabilities in systems and devise ways to exploit them before criminals do.

sentences.en: These guardrails have been widely criticized, particularly by researchers whose job is to find unknown vulnerabilities in systems and devise ways to exploit them before criminals do.
sentences.zh: 这些护栏受到了广泛的批评，特别是受到研究人员的批评，他们的工作是发现系统中未知的漏洞，并在犯罪分子之前设计出利用它们的方法。

blocks.html_en: During a recent appearance on a cybersecurity podcast, Mark Dowd, a well-known security researcher, said that, “it’s not really comfortable to me that these random large companies are making arbitrary decisions about what is safe in security and what’s not.”

sentences.en: During a recent appearance on a cybersecurity podcast, Mark Dowd, a well-known security researcher, said that, “it’s not really comfortable to me that these random large companies are making arbitrary decisions about what is safe in security and what’s not.”
sentences.zh: 著名安全研究员马克·多德 (Mark Dowd) 最近在网络安全播客中表示，“这些随机的大公司对安全方面什么是安全的、什么是不安全的做出武断的决定，这让我感到不太舒服。”

blocks.html_en: Dowd has spent decades finding and selling “zero-days” — previously unknown software flaws and the exploits that take advantage of them — to Western governments, rather than reporting them to the software makers so they get patched. Governments pay a premium for vulnerabilities precisely because they stay open, which is useful for intelligence operations.

sentences.en: Dowd has spent decades finding and selling “zero-days” — previously unknown software flaws and the exploits that take advantage of them — to Western governments, rather than reporting them to the software makers so they get patched.
sentences.zh: 多德花了几十年的时间寻找并向西方政府出售“零日”——以前未知的软件缺陷和利用它们的漏洞——而不是向软件制造商报告这些缺陷以便得到修补。

sentences.en: Governments pay a premium for vulnerabilities precisely because they stay open, which is useful for intelligence operations.
sentences.zh: 政府为漏洞支付高价正是因为它们保持开放状态，这对情报行动很有用。

sentences.en: But if a guardrail prompts the model to refuse to answer the question outright, the guardrail hurts defenders, he said.
sentences.zh: 但如果护栏促使模型拒绝直接回答问题，那么护栏就会伤害防御者，他说。

sentences.en: “So at the same time, the same tool is both an offensive tool and a defensive tool, and the two can’t really be unpicked.”
sentences.zh: “所以同时，同一个工具既是进攻工具，又是防御工具，两者实际上是不可分割的。”

sentences.zh: 它“就像一把锤子，”他继续说道。

sentences.zh: “没有锤子就无法建造房子。

sentences.en: It’s definitely a tool but it’s also irreducibly a weapon as well.”
sentences.zh: 它绝对是一种工具，但它也是一种不可简化的武器。”

sentences.en: “I am jealous of my bugs, and I like this game too much to let models play it for me.”
sentences.zh: “我嫉妒我的 bug，而且我太喜欢这个游戏了，不愿意让模型为我玩。”

sentences.zh: 该人士表示：“如果风吹过来，我们正在做任何与安​​全相关的事情，它就会停止并且无法使用。”

blocks.html_en: Chris Thompson — chief executive of cybersecurity firm RemoteThreat and founder of Offensive AI Con, an offensive security and AI-focused event — said that in his experience using the frontier AI models, the guardrails can be inconsistent and work differently every day. That’s true even inside the looser boundaries of Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s vetted programs.

sentences.en: Chris Thompson — chief executive of cybersecurity firm RemoteThreat and founder of Offensive AI Con, an offensive security and AI-focused event — said that in his experience using the frontier AI models, the guardrails can be inconsistent and work differently every day.
sentences.zh: 网络安全公司 RemoteThreat 的首席执行官兼进攻性 AI Con（一项以进攻性安全和 AI 为重点的活动）的创始人 Chris Thompson 表示，根据他使用前沿 AI 模型的经验，护栏可能会不一致，而且每天的工作方式都不同。

sentences.en: That’s true even inside the looser boundaries of Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s vetted programs.
sentences.zh: 即使在 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 审查程序的较宽松范围内也是如此。

blocks.html_en: “I think the practical impact is you spend a lot of time negotiating with the model instead of working on the core security program,” said Thompson. “Instead of analyzing a vulnerability and reasoning through the exploitability, you’re trying to find why you’re getting inconsistent results or why are models over-sanitizing the output.”

sentences.en: “I think the practical impact is you spend a lot of time negotiating with the model instead of working on the core security program,” said Thompson.
sentences.zh: 汤普森说：“我认为实际影响是你花费大量时间与模型进行谈判，而不是致力于核心安全计划。”

sentences.en: “Instead of analyzing a vulnerability and reasoning through the exploitability, you’re trying to find why you’re getting inconsistent results or why are models over-sanitizing the output.”
sentences.zh: “你不是在分析漏洞并通过可利用性进行推理，而是试图找出为什么会得到不一致的结果，或者为什么模型会对输出进行过度清理。”

blocks.html_en: Consequently, researchers rely on or get pushed toward Chinese open source models like GLM — freely downloadable models that can be run locally with no vetting or usage restrictions — said Thompson.

sentences.en: Consequently, researchers rely on or get pushed toward Chinese open source models like GLM — freely downloadable models that can be run locally with no vetting or usage restrictions — said Thompson.
sentences.zh: Thompson 表示，因此，研究人员依赖或被推向 GLM 等中国开源模型——可免费下载的模型，可以在本地运行，没有审查或使用限制。

blocks.html_en: “You have these responsible researchers that are being pushed away from U.S.-governed systems to foreign-owned systems,” he said. “I think it’s more harmful than good to have these guardrails in place.”

sentences.en: “You have these responsible researchers that are being pushed away from U.S.-governed systems to foreign-owned systems,” he said.
sentences.zh: “这些负责任的研究人员正在从美国管理的系统转移到外资系统，”他说。

sentences.en: “I think it’s more harmful than good to have these guardrails in place.”
sentences.zh: “我认为设置这些护栏弊大于利。”

blocks.html_en: Rather than tightening restrictions further, Thompson called for the AI frontier labs to open up their programs, provide responsible access, and hold those who abuse their tools accountable. Otherwise, he argued, defenders will lose the AI race.

sentences.en: Rather than tightening restrictions further, Thompson called for the AI frontier labs to open up their programs, provide responsible access, and hold those who abuse their tools accountable.
sentences.zh: 汤普森没有进一步收紧限制，而是呼吁人工智能前沿实验室开放他们的项目，提供负责任的访问，并追究那些滥用其工具的人的责任。

sentences.en: Otherwise, he argued, defenders will lose the AI race.
sentences.zh: 他认为，否则，防御者将输掉人工智能竞赛。

blocks.html_en: “There’s this big storm coming. There’s this big wave of attacks that are going to happen at speed and scale like never before,” said Thompson. “But the same security consulting firms and legit researchers that are trying to make a difference are being stifled right now.”

sentences.en: “There’s this big storm coming.
sentences.zh: “一场大风暴即将来临。

sentences.en: There’s this big wave of attacks that are going to happen at speed and scale like never before,” said Thompson.
sentences.zh: 这波攻击将以前所未有的速度和规模发生，”汤普森说。

sentences.en: “But the same security consulting firms and legit researchers that are trying to make a difference are being stifled right now.”
sentences.zh: “但那些试图有所作为的安全咨询公司和合法研究人员现在却遭到了压制。”

blocks.html_en: When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission. This doesn’t affect our editorial independence.

sentences.en: When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission.
sentences.zh: 当您通过我们文章中的链接购买时，我们可能会赚取少量佣金。

sentences.en: This doesn’t affect our editorial independence.
sentences.zh: 这并不影响我们的编辑独立性。

content_html_zh:
几个月来，人工智能巨头制定了经过特殊审查的程序和严格的防护措施，以限制恶意黑客对其模型的使用。 但这些限制现在阻碍了合法网络防御者以及进攻性网络安全研究人员的工作。

6 月，美国政府对 Anthropic 大肆宣传的人工智能模型 Mythos 和 Fable 实施了出口管制限制。 这一举措至少部分是由一份报告推动的，该报告声称可以绕过这些模型的护栏，该护栏旨在防止用户使用它们来构建和执行恶意网络攻击。

不管这起事件是否真的是出于对越狱的恐惧，事实是 Anthropic 一再将 Mythos 推销为某种末日网络机器，只能提供给经过仔细审查的用户，即使如此，也有严格的防护措施。 （《神鬼寓言 5》和《神话 5》的出口管制现已取消。 《神鬼寓言 5》于 7 月 1 日重新开放；作为政府审查流程的一部分，神话 5 仅重新引入经过审查的美国组织。）

这种把关并不是神话独有的。 Anthropic 及其其他模型和 OpenAI 都提供网络安全研究人员计划，他们可以申请进行审查，如果获得批准，则可以访问网络安全限制较少的模型：OpenAI 的可信网络访问计划和 Anthropic 的网络验证计划。

这些护栏受到了广泛的批评，特别是受到研究人员的批评，他们的工作是发现系统中未知的漏洞，并在犯罪分子之前设计出利用它们的方法。

著名安全研究员马克·多德 (Mark Dowd) 最近在网络安全播客中表示，“这些随机的大公司对安全方面什么是安全的、什么是不安全的做出武断的决定，这让我感到不太舒服。”

多德花了几十年的时间寻找并向西方政府出售“零日”——以前未知的软件缺陷和利用它们的漏洞——而不是向软件制造商报告这些缺陷以便得到修补。 政府为漏洞支付高价正是因为它们保持开放状态，这对情报行动很有用。

多德承认他的工作可能会让他产生偏见，但他并不孤单。 一些从事进攻性网络安全工作的人——他们主动探测系统的弱点——向 TechCrunch 描述了他们如何使用人工智能工具和处理护栏。

安全咨询巨头 NCC 集团的首席科学家 Chris Anley 表示，要求 AI 模型尝试利用漏洞是确认该漏洞确实值得修复的关键一步。 但如果护栏促使模型拒绝直接回答问题，那么护栏就会伤害防御者，他说。

安利说：“这就是整个进攻与防御和护栏部分的用武之地，因为‘修复此代码’作为提示既是防御的基本机制，也是在代码库中查找关键漏洞的路线图。” “所以同时，同一个工具既是进攻工具，又是防御工具，两者实际上是不可分割的。”

它“就像一把锤子，”他继续说道。 “没有锤子就无法建造房子。 它绝对是一种工具，但它也是一种不可简化的武器。”

当他和他的同事遇到这样的障碍时，他们有时会求助于根本没有护栏的开源人工智能模型。

Crowdfense 是一家向政府机构开发、收购并向政府机构出售未知漏洞的知名公司，其首席技术官保罗·斯塔尼奥 (Paolo Stagno) 同意多德的观点，称人工智能公司“基本上像对待需要保姆的孩子一样对待客户”，并提供经过审查的程序和护栏。

斯塔尼奥说，他和他的同事确实使用前沿模型——但仅限于逆向工程。 他说，他们避免使用人工智能来帮助发现漏洞或构建漏洞，因为将这项工作输入基于云的模型可能会泄露敏感漏洞数据或将其吸收到未来的训练中。 他说，在这一步中，他们使用本地运行的开源模型，因为他们不依赖于在模型外部共享数据。

发现零日漏洞并开发漏洞的安全研究员朱塞佩·卡利 (Giuseppe Cali) 表示，护栏并没有妨碍他的工作。 那是因为他不会将人工智能用于进攻性工作；而是将人工智能用于进攻性工作。相反，他将其用于初始逆向工程，以理解他正在分析的代码，并构建支持工具。 为此，他表示，人工智能工具可以加快这一过程，让他能够专注于发现漏洞。

“我仍然想自己拥有实际的错误发现和武器化，即使明天所有护栏都被解除，这一点也不会改变，”卡利说。 “我嫉妒我的 bug，而且我太喜欢这个游戏了，不愿意让模型为我玩。”

一家智能手机零部件制造商的一名研究人员因无权接受媒体采访而要求匿名，他表示，他的雇主并未参与 Anthropic 的 CVP 计划，因此，该公司的工具对于发现漏洞几乎没有用处，因为防护栏过于严格。

该人士表示：“如果风吹过来，我们正在做任何与安​​全相关的事情，它就会停止并且无法使用。”

网络安全公司 RemoteThreat 的首席执行官兼进攻性 AI Con（一项以进攻性安全和 AI 为重点的活动）的创始人 Chris Thompson 表示，根据他使用前沿 AI 模型的经验，护栏可能会不一致，而且每天的工作方式都不同。 即使在 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 审查程序的较宽松范围内也是如此。

汤普森说：“我认为实际影响是你花费大量时间与模型进行谈判，而不是致力于核心安全计划。” “你不是在分析漏洞并通过可利用性进行推理，而是试图找出为什么会得到不一致的结果，或者为什么模型会对输出进行过度清理。”

Thompson 表示，因此，研究人员依赖或被推向 GLM 等中国开源模型——可免费下载的模型，可以在本地运行，没有审查或使用限制。

“这些负责任的研究人员正在从美国管理的系统转移到外资系统，”他说。 “我认为设置这些护栏弊大于利。”

汤普森没有进一步收紧限制，而是呼吁人工智能前沿实验室开放他们的项目，提供负责任的访问，并追究那些滥用其工具的人的责任。 他认为，否则，防御者将输掉人工智能竞赛。

“一场大风暴即将来临。 这波攻击将以前所未有的速度和规模发生，”汤普森说。 “但那些试图有所作为的安全咨询公司和合法研究人员现在却遭到了压制。”

当您通过我们文章中的链接购买时，我们可能会赚取少量佣金。 这并不影响我们的编辑独立性。
